_comment: Draft public creative-services page copy. This page should describe genuine public creative services, not DDD-specific supports. Review pricing, funding, privacy, and service boundaries before publishing.

makerSpace.title: Help Build the Studio
makerSpace.body: We are building toward a dedicated maker space — raising funds for equipment, accessibility, and a future community studio. This is a next-phase vision, not a current licensed facility.

meta.title: Creative Project Services & Workshops | Who's to Say ? Foundation
meta.description: Public creative project services and scheduled small-group workshops in digital storytelling, media production, design, audio, and community storytelling.

pricing.title: Session Options
pricing.intro: Pricing reflects session length, format, and materials. Contact us to match the right option to each participant's goals.

tiers.name: Intro Session
tiers.description: A single creative studio visit or short skill-building session to explore fit and interests.

tiers.name: Workshop Block
tiers.description: A focused multi-visit block for a defined project — digital storytelling, design, or studio production.

tiers.name: Extended Project
tiers.description: A longer individualized creative media session series with portfolio or showcase goals.

tiers.name: Community Studio Events
tiers.description: Intergenerational and community participation events as our studio capacity grows.

workshops.title: Digital Storytelling Project
workshops.summary: Plan, record, edit, and share a short participant-owned story or portfolio piece.

workshops.title: Video Editing Basics
workshops.summary: Import, trim, caption, and export short clips using accessible tools.

workshops.title: Podcast & Audio Basics
workshops.summary: Microphone setup, recording, voice practice, and simple editing for audio projects.

workshops.title: Photography & Visual Storytelling
workshops.summary: Compose, capture, and curate images for a personal portfolio or story.

workshops.title: Canva & Digital Design
workshops.summary: Graphics, stickers, flyers, and social visuals with readable layouts.

workshops.title: On-Camera Confidence
workshops.summary: Short intros, teleprompter practice, interview prompts, and presentation clips.

workshops.title: Sports Media Story Project
workshops.summary: Game recaps, fan interviews, trading-card concepts, highlight commentary, or team-story media.

workshops.title: Art, Maker & Showcase Preparation
workshops.summary: Create digital or physical pieces and prepare a low-pressure presentation or gallery-style display.

fundingNote.title: Funding & Third-Party Payment
fundingNote.body: Funding is not guaranteed. Any DDD, plan-based, or third-party-funded purchase must match the individual's documented need and must be approved in writing before services begin. The approved scope should identify the exact service, dates, rate, participant-facing time, any participant-specific technical implementation, and deliverables.

inclusiveSection.title: Public, Inclusive & Project-Based
inclusiveSection.body: These creative services are designed for the broader community and are intentionally accessible to people with and without disabilities. Individualized projects are scoped separately from group workshops, direct support, therapy, case management, employment placement, and personal training.

finalCta.title: Ready to explore a creative project?
finalCta.body: Tell us what you want to make. We'll help determine whether the right next step is a public workshop, an individualized creator project, a mission-funded opportunity, or a referral to another support lane.

hero.title: Creative Project Services & Workshops
hero.subtitle: Individualized creator projects and scheduled small-group workshops in digital storytelling, media production, design, audio, and community storytelling — scoped around real deliverables, not vague life coaching.

classSchedule.title: Workshop Availability
classSchedule.intro: Live workshop availability is paused while we finalize the service catalog, pricing, privacy terms, and funding boundaries. Contact us to discuss a project or future public workshop.

steps.title: Inquire
steps.body: Share the creative goal, preferred format, accessibility needs, timeline, and who should be involved in planning.

steps.title: Scope
steps.body: We define whether the work is a public workshop, an individualized creator project, a mission-funded opportunity, or outside our service lane.

steps.title: Create
steps.body: Sessions focus on the participant-owned project and any approved technical implementation required to complete it.

steps.title: Close Out
steps.body: The participant receives the agreed deliverables, a plain-language handoff, and next-step recommendations when appropriate.
